topic.name: Marine and Fisheries Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents addresses various legislative measures related to marine environments, fisheries management, and pollution control in U.S. waters.

Document 1: designates the Atlantic striped bass as the national fish
Document 4: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to redefine the term 'pollutant'
Document 6: amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act
Document 7: Amends the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 to remove the limit on liability for oil discharge
Document 8: Requires all vessels engaged in drilling for oil or gas to be documented with a registry endorsement

topic.name: Empowerment of Individuals with Disabilities
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing the participation and inclusion of individuals with intellectual disabilities in various community and educational activities.

Document 1: Reauthorizes the Special Olympics Sport and Empowerment Act of 2004
Activities to increase the full participation of people with intellectual disabilities in inclusive school and community activities.
Document 2: Reauthorizes the Special Olympics Sport and Empowerment Act of 2004
Education programs that dispel negative stereotypes about people with intellectual disabilities.
Document 3: Reauthorizes the Special Olympics Sport and Empowerment Act of 2004
Requires annual reports by Special Olympics to describe demographic data about Special Olympics participants.
Document 7: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to award grants to Best Buddies
Promote the expansion of its programs.

topic.name: Outer Continental Shelf Oil Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at prohibiting oil and gas exploration and extraction activities in specific areas of the Outer Continental Shelf.

Document 1: Prohibits oil and gas preleasing, leasing, and related activities in areas of the outer Continental Shelf located off the coast of Mendocino, Humboldt, and Del Norte Counties
Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to exclude such areas from a mandatory inventory of reserves in the Outer Continental Shelf.
Document 2: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from issuing a lease, permit, or license for oil exploration or extraction from specified submerged Outer Continental Shelf lands located within 125 miles of the coastline of New Jersey
including areas with respect to which a moratorium on oil and gas leasing activities existed before enactment of this Act.
Document 5: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to prohibit the conduct of oil and gas preleasing, leasing, and related activities in areas of the outer Continental Shelf located in the North Aleutian Basin Planning Area
Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to exclude such Planning Area from a mandated inventory of oil and natural gas resources beneath the waters of the outer Continental Shelf.
Document 7: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from issuing any oil or gas lease sale under the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act with royalty-based incentives in any tract located in water depths of 400 meters or more on the outer Continental Shelf.
Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to repeal royalty relief (suspension of royalties) for deepwater oil and gas production in the Western and Central Planning Area of the Gulf of Mexico.

topic.name: Surveillance and Courtroom Transparency Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts related to surveillance authority and the transparency of courtroom proceedings in the United States.

1: Amends the USA PATRIOT Improvement and Reauthorization Act of 2005 to extend provisions concerning roving electronic surveillance orders.
Revising the definition of an 'agent of a foreign power' to include non-U.S. persons engaging in international terrorism.
2: Extends expiring provisions of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 concerning FISA court orders approving roving electronic surveillance.
A revised definition of 'agent of a foreign power' allowing surveillance of individual non-U.S. persons.
3: Authorizes the presiding judge to permit the photographing, electronic recording, broadcasting, or televising of court proceedings.
Directs a district court to obscure the face and voice of witnesses upon request.
4: Authorizes the presiding judge to permit the photographing, electronic recording, broadcasting, or televising of court proceedings.
Directs the presiding judge to inform witnesses of their right to request obscuring.
5: Authorizes the presiding judge to permit the photographing, electronic recording, broadcasting, or televising of court proceedings.
Terminates a district court's authority under this Act two years after enactment.
6: Authorizes the presiding judge to permit the photographing, electronic recording, broadcasting, or televising of court proceedings.
Terminates a district court's authority under this Act three years after enactment.
7: Authorizes the presiding judge to permit the photographing, electronic recording, broadcasting, or televising of court proceedings.
Prohibits broadcast of conferences between attorneys and clients.
8: Amends the federal judicial code with respect to the jurisdiction of federal courts over civil actions pending in the U.S. Court of Federal Claims.
Requires the court presiding over the later action to stay the later action until the first action is no longer pending.
9: Amends the federal judicial code with respect to the jurisdiction of federal courts over civil actions pending in the U.S. Court of Federal Claims.
Provides an exception to the requirement to stay the later action if the parties agree.
10: Amends the USA PATRIOT Improvement and Reauthorization Act of 2005 to extend provisions granting roving electronic surveillance authority.
Revises procedures for obtaining judicial review of national security letter nondisclosure orders.

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Legislative Reforms
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative reforms related to Medicare and Medicaid, particularly concerning the roles and responsibilities of healthcare providers and the assignment of beneficiaries to care organizations.

Document 1: Amends part D of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
authorize a Medicare part D eligible individual enrolled in the plan to request an exception to the tiered cost-sharing structure.
Document 2: amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to establish additional requirements for assigning Medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries to accountable care organizations (ACOs)
enables ACOs to receive payments for savings stemming from care coordination and management.
Document 3: amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to establish additional requirements for assigning Medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries to accountable care organizations (ACOs)
requires the basis for assignment to reflect beneficiaries' utilization of not only primary care services provided by ACO physicians.
Document 4: prohibit payment at the applicable site neutral payment rate for discharges in a cost reporting period beginning before October 1, 2017
study the treatment needs of individuals entitled to benefits under Medicare part A.
Document 5: amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to establish additional requirements for assigning Medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries to accountable care organizations (ACOs)
requires the basis for assignment to reflect beneficiaries' utilization of not only primary care services provided by ACO physicians.
Document 6: amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to eliminate the state option to include nurse practitioners, certified nurse-midwives, and physician assistants as primary care case managers
includes nurse practitioners, clinical nurse specialists, physician assistants, certified nurse midwives, and certified registered nurse anesthetists in the mix of service providers.
Document 7: amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to eliminate the state option to include nurse practitioners, certified nurse-midwives, and physician assistants as primary care case managers
specifies as primary care case managers any nurse practitioner, certified nurse-midwife, or physician assistant.
Document 8: amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to eliminate the state option to include nurse practitioners, certified nurse-midwives, and physician assistants as primary care case managers
revises the coverage of certain nurse practitioner services under the Medicaid fee-for-service program.
Document 9: amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to eliminate the state option to include nurse practitioners, certified nurse-midwives, and physician assistants as primary care case managers
specifies as primary care case managers any nurse practitioner, certified nurse-midwife, or physician assistant.

includes nurse practitioners, clinical nurse specialists, physician assistants, certified nurse midwives, and certified registered nurse anesthetists in the mix of service providers.

topic.name: Unemployment Compensation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts aimed at extending and modifying unemployment compensation programs in the United States.

Document 1: Amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) (OASDI) of the Social Security Act to revise the formula for the computation of minimum Old Age Insurance benefits.
Sets forth a schedule of additional benefit increases for such beneficiaries.
Document 2: Extends the Emergency Unemployment Compensation (EUC) program through December 31, 2010.

Amends the Assistance for Unemployed Workers and Struggling Families Act to extend until January 1, 2011, federal-state agreements increasing regular unemployment compensation payments to individuals.
Document 3: Requires a further additional Tier-5 period for deposits to an individual's EUCA if...
Prescribes a formula for determining if a state is in an extended benefit period.
Document 4: Extends emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals to weeks of employment ending on or before January 1, 2015.
Amends the Assistance for Unemployed Workers and Struggling Families Act to extend until December 31, 2014, requirements that federal payments to states cover 100% of EUC.
Document 5: Extends emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals to weeks of employment ending on or before January 1, 2015.
Amends the Assistance for Unemployed Workers and Struggling Families Act to extend until December 31, 2014, requirements that federal payments to states cover 100% of EUC.
Document 6: Extends emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals to weeks of employment ending on or before January 1, 2015.
Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 to appropriate funds out of the employment security administration account through FY2015.
Document 7: Extends the final date for entering a federal-state agreement under the Emergency Unemployment Compensation (EUC) program through January 3, 2012.
Postpones the termination of the program until June 9, 2012.
Document 8: Revises the formula for Tier-1 amounts a state credits to an applicant's emergency unemployment compensation account (EUCA) for a benefit year.
Extends the period of emergency unemployment compensation.
Document 9: Revises the formula for Tier-1 amounts a state credits to an applicant's emergency unemployment compensation account (EUCA) for a benefit year.
Extends the period of emergency unemployment compensation through August 27, 2009.
Document 10: Extends emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals to weeks of employment ending on or before April 1, 2014.
Amends the Railroad Unemployment Insurance Act to extend through March 31, 2014, the temporary increase in extended unemployment benefits.

topic.name: Student Loan Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative measures aimed at reforming and improving student loan policies and protections for borrowers.

Document 1: Protecting Our Students by Terminating Graduate Rates that Add to Debt Act
amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965
Document 2: Student Loan Fair Prepayment Act
requires a student loan borrower's prepayment amount on a Federal Family Education Loan, Federal Direct Loan, or Federal Perkins Loan to be applied first toward outstanding fees
Document 3: Year-Round Pell Grant Restoration Act
requires the Department of Education to award an additional Pell Grant to a student who has received a Pell Grant for an award year
Document 4: Education for Jobs Act
makes an individual eligible to apply for a loan under the William D. Ford Federal Direct Loan program
Document 5: Ensure Responsible Borrowing Act
modifies loan counseling requirements for an institution of higher education
Document 6: Christopher Bryski Student Loan Protection Act
requires lenders of private educational loans for which cosigners are held jointly liable to describe clearly and conspicuously, in writing, the cosigners' obligations
Document 7: Parent PLUS Loan Improvement Act of 2016
modifies the applicable terms and conditions with respect to Federal Direct PLUS Loans to parent borrowers
Document 8: Christopher Bryski Student Loan Protection Act
requires lenders of private education loans to describe clearly and conspicuously, in writing, the cosigners' obligations
Document 9: College Debt Swap Act of 2011
allows certain borrowers to consolidate their private education loans as Direct Consolidation Loans
Document 10: Christopher Bryski Student Loan Protection Act
requires institutions of higher education to provide borrowers of federal education loans information at their entrance counseling

topic.name: Aviation Safety Regulations
topic.description: The documents focus on regulations related to aviation safety, specifically concerning access control to aircraft flight decks.

Document 1: Directs the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to issue an order to require: (1) the installation of secondary barriers to prevent access to the flight deck of a commercial aircraft;

with respect to an aircraft that is equipped with a cockpit door, that such barriers remain locked while the aircraft is in flight and the cockpit door separating the flight deck and the passenger area is open.
Document 4: Saracini Aviation Safety Act of 2013 - Directs the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to issue an order to require: (1) the installation of secondary barriers, other than the cockpit door, to prevent access to the flight deck of each commercial aircraft;
such barriers on an aircraft with a cockpit door to remain locked while the aircraft is in flight and the cockpit door separating the flight deck and the passenger area is open.

topic.name: Child Protection and Law Enforcement
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing child protection, combating sexual violence, and improving law enforcement responses to related crimes.

1: Anti-Gang Task Force Act of 2009 directs the Attorney General to establish anti-gang task forces.
Coordination efforts to combat criminal gangs and offenders.
2: Amends the Missing Children's Assistance Act to provide technical assistance to law enforcement agencies.
Identifying, locating, and recovering victims of child sex trafficking.
3: Child Protection Reauthorization Act of 2009 extends funding for sex offender management programs.
Grants to combat sexual abuse of children.
4: Grants to Parents For Megan's Law to implement the Sex Offender Registration Tips Program.
Program established to reduce sexual victimization by providing accurate sex offender registry information.
5: Drug Endangered Children Act of 2007 extends grant program for drug-endangered children.
6: Child Protection Reauthorization Act of 2008 extends authorization of appropriations for sex offender management programs.
Grants to assist states in enforcing sex offender registration requirements.
7: Justice for Native Survivors of Sexual Violence Act expands tribal jurisdiction over domestic violence crimes.
Includes provisions for sex trafficking and sexual violence.
8: A Child Is Missing Alert and Recovery Center Act directs grants to assist in recovering missing children.
Supports federal, state, and local law enforcement agencies.
9: A Child Is Missing Alert and Recovery Center Act directs grants to assist in recovering missing children.
Supports federal, state, and local law enforcement agencies.
10: Debbie Smith Reauthorization Act of 2008 authorizes appropriations for DNA analysis grant programs.
Reauthorizes grant programs for sexual assault forensic exams.

topic.name: Renewable Fuel Standards and Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at regulating and promoting the use of renewable fuels in transportation, including biofuels and alternative fuels.

Document 1: Consumer Fuels and Vehicle Choice Act of 2009 - Requires each manufacturer to ensure that at least 50% of 2011 and 2012 model year automobiles and light duty trucks manufactured for sale in the United States are dual fueled.
Requires the Secretary of Energy to make grants to major fuel distributors to pay the federal share of costs to install blender pump fuel infrastructure.
Document 3: RFS Reform Act of 2015 - This bill amends the Clean Air Act to revise the renewable fuel standard program.
Beginning on January 1, 2015, the renewable fuel that is required to be blended into gasoline must be advanced biofuel.
Document 5: Amends the Clean Air Act to revise the renewable fuel program by: (1) requiring the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to ensure that transportation fuel sold or introduced into commerce in the United States, on an annual average basis, contains at least the applicable volume of 'next generation biofuel.'
Defines 'next generation biofuel' to mean: renewable fuel that is derived from any cellulose, hemicellulose, lignin, or algae.
Document 6: Domestic Alternative Fuels Act of 2013 - Amends the Clean Air Act to revise the Renewable Fuel Program to: (1) provide that the applicable volume of renewable fuel required in transportation fuel sold or introduced into commerce in the United States for 2013 through 2022 shall apply in the aggregate to renewable fuel and domestic alternative fuel.

topic.name: Labor Rights and Representation
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the National Labor Relations Act focusing on employee rights, union representation, and collective bargaining processes.

Document 1: Amends the National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) to make it an unlawful labor practice for a labor organization or its agents to interfere with the rights of employees to organize and select representation to bargain collectively.
Adds a requirement that representatives be selected by secret ballot in an election conducted by the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) by a majority of the employees in a unit.
Document 2: Amends the National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) to make it an unlawful labor practice for a labor organization or its agents to interfere with the rights of employees to organize and select representation to collectively bargain.
Adds a requirement that representatives be selected by secret ballot in an election conducted by the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) by a majority of the employees in a unit.
Document 4: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) to certify a bargaining representative without directing an election if a majority of the bargaining unit employees have authorized designation of the representative (card-check).
Revises enforcement requirements with respect to unfair labor practices during union organizing drives.

topic.name: Consumer Data Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing the protection and transparency of consumer personal information handled by financial and data broker entities.

Document 1: Amends the Consumer Financial Protection Act of 2010 to direct the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) to disclose to a consumer all information held by the CFPB with respect to that consumer.
Permits consumers to request such a disclosure: online, via U.S. mail, and via a toll-free telephone number.
Document 3: Do-Not-Track Online Act of 2011 - Requires the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to promulgate regulations that establish standards for the implementation of a mechanism by which an individual can indicate whether he or she prefers to have personal information collected.
Prohibits such providers from collecting personal information on individuals who have expressed a preference not to have such information collected.
Document 4: Requires the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to promulgate regulations requiring each person engaged in interstate commerce that owns or possesses data containing personal information to establish specified security policies and procedures to treat and protect such information.
Makes it unlawful for information brokers to obtain or disclose personal information by false pretenses.
Document 7: Prohibits data brokers from obtaining or causing to be disclosed personal information by making a false, fictitious, or fraudulent statement or representation.
Requires data brokers to establish procedures to ensure the accuracy of the personal information they collect.

topic.name: Tax Incentives for Small Businesses
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at providing tax benefits and credits to small businesses to encourage innovation, investment, and economic growth.

Document 10: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase the exclusion from gross income of the gain from the sale or exchange of qualified small business stock from 50 to 100% of such gain.
Increase to $100 million the aggregate asset limitation for determining eligibility of the stock of corporations for qualified small business stock tax treatment.

topic.name: Water Quality and Invasive Species Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at improving water quality and managing invasive species in aquatic ecosystems.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to reauthorize appropriations for coastal recreation water quality monitoring and notification grants
reauthorize appropriations to carry out that Act through FY 2012.
Document 2: This bill amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to reauthorize through FY2020 the drinking water state revolving fund program.
reauthorize through FY2020 the state revolving fund program for water pollution control.
Document 4: This bill requires federal agencies to take immediate actions to prevent the upstream transfer of aquatic nuisance species from the Mississippi River basin to the Great Lakes basin
with a focus on Asian carp species and other aquatic nuisance species of concern.
Document 6: Requires the Director of the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service to lead a multiagency effort to slow the spread of Asian Carp
by providing support to state and local governments in carrying out activities designed to slow, and eventually eliminate, the threat posed by such carp.
Document 9: Establishes a nutria eradication program under which the Secretary of the Interior may provide financial assistance to states to eradicate or control nutria
and restore nutria-damaged wetland.

topic.name: U.S. Legislation on Terrorism and Sanctions
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislative measures addressing the use of cluster munitions and imposing sanctions on countries identified as supporters of terrorism.

Document 1: Places specified restrictions on the use of cluster munitions.

Document 2: Places specified restrictions on the use of cluster munitions.

Document 3: Directs the Secretary of State to report to Congress on Boko Haram's designation as a foreign terrorist organization.

Document 4: Continues diplomatic, economic, and military sanctions against North Korea as a supporter of international terrorism.

Document 5: Maintains U.S. restrictions against Syria until specified conditions are met.

Imposes specified sanctions aimed at Syria's energy sector.
Document 7: Continues sanctions against North Korea until the President certifies compliance with specified benchmarks.
Directs the President to secure adoption of a Security Council resolution condemning North Korea's actions.
Document 8: Continues sanctions against North Korea until the President certifies compliance with specified benchmarks.
Directs the President to secure adoption of a Security Council resolution condemning North Korea's actions.
Document 9: Continues sanctions against North Korea as a supporter of international terrorism until specific conditions are met.
Expresses the sense of Congress that North Korea should be redesignated as a state sponsor of terrorism.
Document 10: Continues sanctions against North Korea until specified conditions are met.
Expresses the sense of Congress regarding North Korea's state sponsor of terrorism designation.

topic.name: Regulatory Accountability and Transparency
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative efforts aimed at enhancing the accountability, transparency, and impact analysis of federal regulations.

Document 1: adds definitions to the Administrative Procedure Act
rules remain subject to general notice of proposed rulemaking
Document 2: requires federal agencies to submit monthly reports
must include cost and economic effects of rules
Document 3: revises the definition of 'agency' for federal mandates
includes independent regulatory agencies
Document 4: prohibits issuing new rules unless existing rules are repealed
requires publication in the Federal Register
Document 5: authorizes the President to require compliance with regulatory analysis
prohibits judicial review of compliance
Document 6: requires regulatory impact analysis prior to promulgating rules
extends UMRA requirements to independent regulatory agencies
Document 7: revises rulemaking requirements under UMRA
expands judicial review of agency rulemaking
Document 8: requires initial and final regulatory impact analysis
defines 'cost' as compliance costs
Document 9: requires regulatory impact analyses for significant rules
expands provisions relating to judicial review
Document 10: authorizes the President to require independent agencies to comply with regulatory analysis
requires assessment of costs and benefits of proposed rules

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: This topic encompasses various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at altering taxpayer deductions and reporting requirements.

Document 1: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is Act of 2009 - Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow taxpayers to designate on their tax returns a payment of $1 or more to the general fund of the Treasury.

Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is Act of 2011 - Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow taxpayers to designate on their income tax returns a payment of $1 or more to the general fund of the Treasury.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the election to deduct state and local sales taxes in lieu of state and local income taxes.
Document 9: Taxpayer Responsibility, Accountability and Consistency Act of 2009 - Amends the Internal Revenue Code to: (1) require reporting to the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) of payments of $600 or more made to corporations; (2) set forth criteria and rules relating to the treatment of workers as employees or independent contractors; and (3) increase penalties for failure to file correct tax return information or comply with other information reporting requirements.

topic.name: Federal Budget Accountability
topic.description: Legislation aimed at ensuring accountability and transparency in the allocation and expenditure of federal budget resources.

Document 1: Requires the total budget resources for expenditures from the Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund to equal the level of receipts plus interest.
Limits the use of such resources to harbor maintenance programs only.
Document 2: Exempts expenditures derived from user fees from the prohibition against exceeding available amounts in an appropriation.
Document 4: Prohibits a federal agency from obligating funds for an earmark unless included in the Act.
Document 5: Allows unobligated balances to remain available for expenditure in subsequent fiscal years.
Document 7: Excludes receipts and disbursements of the Crime Victims Fund from executive and congressional budgets.

topic.name: Health Insurance Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts that amend or repeal provisions of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act and other related health insurance regulations.

Document 1: Repeals provisions of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (PPACA) that prohibit a health plan offering group or individual health insurance coverage from rescinding coverage once an enrollee is covered.
require prior notice to an enrollee before coverage is cancelled.
Document 2: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA), the Public Health Service Act, and the Internal Revenue Code to prohibit a group health plan from imposing any preexisting condition exclusion for individuals who have not attained 25 years of age.
Applies such requirement to coverage offered in the individual market.
Document 3: Repeals provisions of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (PPACA) that require a health plan offering group or individual health insurance coverage that provides dependent coverage of children to continue to make such coverage available until the child turns 26 years old.
Document 4: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA), the Public Health Service Act, and the Internal Revenue Code to prohibit a group health plan from imposing any preexisting condition exclusion for individuals who have not attained 19 years of age.
Applies such requirement to coverage offered in the individual market.
Document 5: Repeals provisions of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act that restrict payments from health savings accounts, medical savings accounts, and health flexible spending arrangements for medications to prescription drugs and insulin only.
thus allowing payments for over-the-counter medications.
Document 6: Repeals provisions of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (PPACA) and the Health Care and Education Reconciliation Act of 2010 that require individuals to maintain minimum essential health care coverage.
require large employers to offer their full-time employees the opportunity to enroll in minimum essential coverage.
Document 7: Repeals provisions of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act and the Health Care and Education Reconciliation Act of 2010 that restrict payments from health savings accounts, medical savings accounts, and health flexible spending arrangements for medications to prescription drugs and insulin only.
impose a $2,500 limitation on salary reduction contributions to a health flexible spending arrangement under a cafeteria plan.
Document 8: Permits a health insurance issuer that had in effect health insurance coverage in the group market on any date during 2013 to continue offering that coverage through 2018 outside of a health care exchange established under the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act.
Treats that coverage as a grandfathered health plan for purposes of an individual meeting the requirement to maintain minimum essential health coverage.
Document 9: Repeals provisions of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act and the Health Care and Education Reconciliation Act of 2010 that restrict payments from health savings accounts, medical savings accounts, and health flexible spending arrangements for medications to prescription drugs and insulin only.
thus allowing payments for over-the-counter medications.
Document 10: This bill amends the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act to allow catastrophic plans to be offered as qualified health plans to any individual in the individual or group market.
to be a qualified health plan, a catastrophic plan must enroll only individuals who are below age 30 and exempt from minimum essential coverage requirements.

topic.name: Public Health Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving health care services and awareness for various medical conditions and populations.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to authorize the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants to public and nonprofit private health care providers to expand treatment services for patients with Alzheimer's disease and training and support services for families and caregivers of such patients.
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to allow the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants to public and nonprofit private health care providers to provide treatment for diabetes in minority communities.
Document 4: This bill amends the Public Health Service Act to prohibit the use of tissue from a spontaneous or induced abortion in research conducted or supported by the National Institutes of Health on the transplantation of fetal tissue for therapeutic purposes.
Document 5: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require mammography facilities to include information regarding the patient's individual measure of breast density in both the written report of the results of a mammography examination provided to the patient's physician.

topic.name: Federal Agency Accountability and Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses the evaluation, accountability, and reform of federal agencies to improve efficiency and effectiveness in government operations.

Document 1: Establishes the Commission on Congressional Budgetary Accountability and Review of Federal Agencies.
Requires the Commission to evaluate executive agencies and their programs.
Document 2: Amends the Government Performance and Results Act of 1993 to require each executive agency to make its strategic plan available on its public website.
Requires such plan to cover at least a four-year period and to include a description of how the agency is working with other agencies to achieve its goals.
Document 3: Establishes the Independent Government Waste Reduction Board.
Sets forth the duties of the Board, including assessing federal programs for economy, efficiency, and effectiveness.
Document 4: Directs the Commission to review work done by governmental and non-governmental entities and conduct research on the organizational practices, operations, and reform efforts of federal agencies.
Assess federal programs for economy, efficiency, and effectiveness.
Document 5: Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to create a separate website about the reports issued by each Office of Inspector General.
Includes a general description of the role of the Office of Inspector General.
Document 6: Evaluates executive agencies and their programs.
Submits to Congress a plan recommending agencies and programs that should be realigned or eliminated.
Document 7: Directs the Commission to analyze organizational practices and management challenges of federal agencies.
Requires the Commission to make interim and annual reports on its activities to the President and Congress.
Document 8: Establishes a systematic method for assessing the effectiveness and accountability of agency programs.
Submit to Congress each of the next four years a plan recommending agencies and programs that should be realigned or eliminated.
Document 9: Requires the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to create a separate website about the reports issued by each Office of Inspector General.
Includes a description of the types of reports issued by the Office of Inspector General.
Document 10: Establishes the Commission on Congressional Budgetary Accountability and Review of Federal Agencies.
Requires the Commission to submit to Congress a plan recommending agencies and programs that should be realigned or eliminated.

topic.name: Military Detention and Legal Authority
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures and discussions surrounding the detention of individuals under U.S. military authority, particularly in relation to the Authorization for Use of Military Force and the Guantanamo Bay detention facility.

Document 1: U.S. Naval Station, Guantanamo Bay, Cuba is a location for the detention of individuals in custody or control of the Department of Defense.
Prohibits any federal funds from being used to transfer any individual detained at Guantanamo.
Document 2: Repeals the Authorization for Use of Military Force (P.L. 107-40), effective 180 days after the date of enactment of this Act.
States that the open-ended authorization for the use of military force is inconsistent with the authority of Congress.
Document 5: Disposition under the law of war shall occur immediately upon custody and shall only mean transfer for trial and proceedings by a court established under article III of the Constitution.
Prohibits any person detained in the United States from being transferred to the custody of the Armed Forces for detention under the Authorization for Use of Military Force.
Document 7: Requires the President to close the Department of Defense detention facility at Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.
Requires all current detainees to be transferred to a military or civilian detention facility in the United States.
Document 9: Prohibits the use of funds to transfer, release, or assist in the transfer or release of any detainee who is not a U.S. citizen or a member of the Armed Forces.
No funds may be used to close or abandon Guantanamo.

topic.name: Transportation System Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the development and regulation of public and multimodal transportation systems in urban and regional contexts.

Document 1: Transit System Flexibility Protection Act of 2007
Authorizes the Secretary of Transportation to make public transportation formula grants for operating costs of equipment and facilities for use in public transportation.
Document 3: Aerotropolis Act of 2010
Extends the eligibility for projects of national and regional significance under the surface transportation grant program.
Document 5: Aerotropolis Act of 2015
Defines 'aerotropolis transportation system' as a planned and coordinated multimodal freight and passenger transportation network.

topic.name: Water Resource Management
topic.description: The documents collectively address legislative acts related to the planning, design, and construction of water recycling, wastewater treatment, and water conservation projects across various regions in the United States.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to carry out the project entitled the 'Dry-Redwater Regional Water Authority System'
Limits the federal share of planning, design, and construction of the System to 75% of the total cost.
Document 2: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior
Prohibits the use of funds provided by the Secretary under this Act for project operation or maintenance.
Document 3: This bill amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Department of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of recycled water system facilities
The federal share of each project is limited to 25%.
Document 4: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to amend an existing contract or enter into one or more new contracts with the Central Oklahoma Master Conservancy District
Makes the costs of constructing, operating, and maintaining any additional infrastructure needed... the responsibility of the non-federal entity.
Document 5: This bill amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Department of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of recycled water system facilities
The federal share of each project is limited to 25%.
Document 6: Amends the Lower Rio Grande Valley Water Resources Conservation and Improvement Act of 2000 to authorize specified additional projects
Permits each project that the Secretary of the Interior conducts to include... activities that will result in water conservation.
Document 7: Amends the Lower Rio Grande Valley Water Resources Conservation and Improvement Act of 2000 to authorize specified additional projects
Permits each project that the Secretary of the Interior conducts to include... activities that will result in water conservation.
Document 8: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of recycled water distribution systems
Limits the federal share of each project to 25%.
Document 9: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of recycled water distribution systems
Limits the federal share of each project to 25%.
Document 10: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of recycled water system facilities
Limits the federal share of each project to 25%.

topic.name: Regulation of Greenhouse Gases
topic.description: This cluster of documents primarily addresses legislative measures aimed at regulating greenhouse gas emissions and modifying the definitions and roles associated with the Clean Air Act and the EPA.

1: Prohibits any funds appropriated or otherwise available for the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from being used to implement or enforce: (1) a cap-and-trade program; or (2) any statutory or regulatory requirement pertaining to emissions of one or more greenhouse gases.

Defines: (1) 'cap-and-trade program' as any regulatory program established after the date of enactment of this Act that provides for the sale, auction, or other distribution of a limited amount of allowances that permit the emission of one or more greenhouse gases.
3: Amends the Clean Air Act to prohibit the issuance of permits under such Act for any carbon dioxide, nitrogen oxide, water vapor, or methane emissions resulting from biological processes associated with livestock production.
4: Prohibits the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from proposing any standard of performance under the Clean Air Act for carbon dioxide emissions from existing fossil fuel-fired electric utility generating units before finalizing and publishing a standard of performance for such emissions from new fossil fuel-fired electric utility generating units.
5: Amends the Clean Air Act to: (1) exclude from the definition of the term 'air pollutant' carbon dioxide, water vapor, methane, nitrous oxide, hydrofluorocarbons, perfluorocarbons, or sulfur hexafluoride; and (2) declare that nothing in the Act shall be treated as authorizing or requiring the regulation of climate change or global warming.
7: Amends the Clean Air Act to: (1) exclude from the definition of the term 'air pollutant' carbon dioxide, water vapor, methane, or chlorofluorocarbons; and (2) declare that nothing in the Act authorizes or requires the regulation of climate change or global warming.

topic.name: Employment Discrimination Legislation
topic.description: The documents discuss various U.S. legislative acts aimed at preventing employment discrimination based on gender, pregnancy, and sexual orientation.

1: Declares it an unlawful employment practice for employers... to fail to make reasonable accommodations to known limitations related to pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions.
Directs the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC) to issue regulations to carry out this Act.
2: Amends the portion of the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 (FLSA) known as the Equal Pay Act to revise remedies for, enforcement of, and exceptions to prohibitions against sex discrimination in the payment of wages.
Prohibits retaliation for inquiring about, discussing, or disclosing the wages of the employee or another employee.
3: Prohibits employment discrimination on the basis of actual or perceived sexual orientation or gender identity by covered entities.
Makes this Act inapplicable to religious organizations and the relationship between the United States and members of the Armed Forces.

topic.name: Family Immigration Policies
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to the immigration status and rights of families, particularly concerning orphans, widows, and family reunification.

Document 1: Orphans, Widows, and Widowers Protection Act - Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to revise the definition of 'immediate relative'...
Provides specified relief for orphans and spouses regarding...
Document 2: Expedited Family Reunification Act of 2014 - Amends the William Wilberforce Trafficking Victims Protection Reauthorization Act...
Directs the Secretary of State to negotiate agreements...
Document 4: Reuniting Families Act - Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to establish the fiscal year worldwide level of family-sponsored immigrants...
Revises the definition of 'immediate relative' to...
Document 5: Protection of Children Act of 2016 - This bill amends the William Wilberforce Trafficking Victims Protection Reauthorization Act...
An immigration officer who finds an inadmissible UAC at a land border or port of entry shall return the child...
Document 6: Reuniting Families Act - Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to establish the fiscal year worldwide level of employment-based immigrants...
Increases immigration visas for...
Document 10: Reuniting Families Act - Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to establish the fiscal year worldwide level of family-sponsored immigrants...
Revises the definition of 'immediate relative' to...

topic.name: Military Retirement Benefits
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts related to military retirement pay, disability compensation, and benefits for military families and surviving spouses.

Document 1: Allows the receipt of both military retired pay and veterans' disability compensation with respect to any service-connected disability.

Makes eligible for the full concurrent receipt of both veterans' disability compensation and either military retired pay or combat-related special pay those individuals who were retired or separated from military service due to a service-connected disability.
Document 2: Authorizes space-available travel on military aircraft for members of the reserves.
Authorizes such travel for unremarried widows and widowers of retired members entitled to retired pay.
Document 5: Requires that a member of a reserve component of the Armed Forces who was deployed for more than 179 days be retained on active duty for 45 days after demobilization.
Directs the Secretary to provide each member retained reintegration counseling and services.
Document 8: Repeals certain provisions which require the offset of amounts paid in dependency and indemnity compensation from Survivor Benefit Plan (SBP) annuities.
Directs the Secretary concerned to restore annuity eligibility to a surviving spouse who earlier agreed to transfer such eligibility to a surviving child.

topic.name: Tariff Suspension on Acrylic Products
topic.description: The documents discuss the temporary suspension of duties on various acrylic and modacrylic textile products under the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

1: suspend temporarily the duty on acrylic filament tow
imported in the form of bundles of crimped product
2: suspend temporarily the duty on acrylic staple fibers
not carded, combed, or otherwise processed for spinning
3: suspend temporarily the duty on acrylic filament tow
imported in the form of bundles of crimped product
4: suspend temporarily the duty on certain men's wading boots
5: extends and modifies the temporary reduction of duty on certain women's footwear
designed to be worn in lieu of other footwear
6: suspend temporarily the duty on certain men's wading boots
7: suspend temporarily the duty on modacrylic staple fibers
containing 35 percent or more but not over 85 percent by weight of acrylonitrile units
8: extend the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic staple fibers
containing at least 85 percent by weight of acrylonitrile units
9: extend the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic staple fibers
containing 85 percent or more by weight of acrylonitrile units
10: extend the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic staple fibers
containing at least 85 percent by weight of acrylonitrile units

topic.name: Intelligence Authorization Acts
topic.description: Legislation authorizing appropriations and personnel for U.S. intelligence activities across various federal agencies.

Document 1: Authorizes appropriations for FY2008 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings as of September 30, 2008, for such activities are those specified in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations for FY2010 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings as of September 30, 2010, for such activities are those specified in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 3: Authorizes appropriations for FY2009 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Allows the DNI, with the approval of the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB), to authorize employment of civilian personnel in excess of the number authorized for FY2009.
Document 5: Authorizes appropriations for FY2008 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Allows the DNI to convert to performance by intelligence community (IC) personnel activities currently performed by contractor employees.
Document 7: Authorizes appropriations for FY2011 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings as of September 30, 2011, for such activities are those specified in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 10: Authorizes appropriations for FY2007 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Requires notification of the congressional intelligence committees on the use of such authority.

topic.name: Broadcasting and Communication Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts that amend the Communications Act of 1934, focusing on broadcasting rights, political communication regulations, and retransmission requirements for television and mobile communication services.

1: prohibit the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) from having the authority to require broadcasters to present opposing viewpoints on controversial issues
3: Freedom from Automated Political Calls Act
prohibit politically-oriented recorded message telephone calls to telephone numbers listed on that registry
4: Ending Mobile Phone Welfare Act of 2015
prohibits a provider of commercial mobile communications service from receiving universal service support
5: requires statutory licensing for a satellite carrier's secondary transmission into a television broadcast station's adjacent market
9: Granting Our People the Ability to Cheer Kickoff Act of 2015
require a cable operator or satellite carrier to provide to certain subscribers retransmission of the signal of any local network station
10: Granting Our People Access to Channel Choice Act of 2014
require a cable operator or satellite carrier to provide to certain subscribers retransmission of the signal of any local network station

topic.name: Election Reform Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts aimed at reforming election processes and campaign finance in the United States.

1: Amends the Revised Statutes with respect to the time of election to establish the first Saturday and Sunday after the first Friday in November, in every even numbered year, as the days for the election.
Establishes the same polling place hours in the United States for both congressional and presidential elections.
3: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to redefine the term 'independent expenditure' as an expenditure by a person that, when taken as a whole, expressly advocates the election or defeat of a clearly identified candidate.
Requires any communication transmitted through radio or television to include an individual or organizational disclosure statement.
4: Treats the District of Columbia as a state for purposes of representation in the House of Representatives and in the Senate.
Increases membership of the House from 435 to 436 Members.
6: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more to the Federal Election Commission.
Requires Senate candidates to file designations, statements, and reports directly with the FEC.
9: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to redefine the term 'independent expenditure' as an expenditure by a person that, when taken as a whole, expressly advocates the election or defeat of a clearly identified candidate.
Prescribes disclosure requirements for corporations, labor organizations, and certain other entities.

topic.name: High School Improvement Initiatives
topic.description: Legislative acts focused on improving high school graduation rates and supporting struggling schools through targeted interventions and funding.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to make grants to states for differentiated high school improvement systems targeting support to schools with low student achievement and graduation rates.
Requires state grantees to establish comprehensive school performance indicators and minimum annual improvement benchmarks.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to make grants to states for differentiated high school improvement systems targeting support to schools with low student achievement and graduation rates.
Directs LEA subgrantees to categorize each of their schools that fail to make AYP for two consecutive years as needing targeted intervention.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to make grants to states for differentiated high school improvement systems targeting support to schools with low student achievement and graduation rates.
Authorizes the Secretary to award competitive grants to LEAs, nonprofit organizations, and institutions of higher education to develop and implement effective secondary school models.
Document 4: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to make grants to states for differentiated high school improvement systems targeting support to schools with low student achievement and graduation rates.
Directs LEA subgrantees to support the successful implementation of such plans and district-wide high school improvement strategies.
Document 6: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to make matching grants to states for differentiated high school improvement systems targeting support to schools with low student achievement and graduation rates.
Requires state grantees to establish comprehensive school performance indicators and minimum annual improvement benchmarks.

topic.name: Wilderness Designation and Management
topic.description: Legislation focused on designating and managing wilderness areas across various states in the U.S., emphasizing conservation, recreation, and cultural resources.

1: Amends the Colorado Wilderness Act of 1993 to add specified lands in Colorado managed by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) to the Powderhorn Wilderness.
Designates specified lands in Colorado as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System (NWPS).
2: Designates specified federal land managed by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) in Humboldt County, Nevada, as a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System (NWPS).
Sets forth provisions for the management of such land, including regarding livestock grazing and water rights.
3: Designates specified federal land managed by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) in Humboldt County, Nevada, as wilderness and as a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System (NWPS).
Authorizes land exchanges involving identified federal and non-federal lands in Humboldt County.
4: Establishes the Organ Mountains monument in Dona Ana County, New Mexico, for the conservation and enhancement of cultural, natural, and recreational resources.
Directs the Secretary of the Interior to manage the monument in a manner to conserve, protect, and enhance those resources.
5: Establishes the Browns Canyon National Monument in Colorado to preserve significant biological, cultural, and recreational values.
Requires the Secretary of the Interior to complete a comprehensive management plan for the conservation and protection of the National Monument and the Wilderness.
6: Amends the Colorado Wilderness Act of 1993 to add specified lands in Colorado to the Powderhorn Wilderness.
Designates specified lands as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
7: Amends the Colorado Wilderness Act of 1993 to add specified lands in Colorado to the Powderhorn Wilderness.
Designates specified lands in Colorado as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
8: Designates areas in California as wilderness areas and as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Releases any parts of certain areas from further study for designation as wilderness.
9: Designates as wilderness certain Bureau of Land Management (BLM) lands in California as part of various wilderness areas.
Requires the Secretary of the Interior to manage such land as wilderness until it is incorporated into the designated wilderness.
10: Designates lands in California as wilderness as part of various wilderness areas.
Provides for access to certain wilderness areas by Indian tribes for traditional cultural purposes.

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative amendments aimed at regulating financial institutions, capital requirements, and the relationships between banks, insurance companies, and securities entities.

1: Amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act with respect to establishment of minimum leverage and minimum risk-based capital requirements
Federal banking agencies shall not be required to subject to such minimum requirements any person, to the extent that person acts in its capacity as a regulated insurance entity.
2: Amends the Consumer Financial Protection Act of 2010 to raise the examination threshold
Increases from assets of $10 billion or less to assets of $50 billion or less the size of an insured depository institution.
4: Amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 to prohibit a bank holding company from holding more than 10% of the total amount of deposits
Prescribes leverage ratio requirements for operating subsidiaries of bank holding companies.
5: Amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act concerning establishment of minimum leverage and minimum risk-based capital requirements
Exempts from any requirement to prepare holding company financial statements in accordance with Generally Accepted Accounting Principles.
7: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to prohibit an insured depository institution from being or becoming an affiliate of any insurance company
Prohibits any individual who is an officer, director, partner, or employee of any securities entity from serving simultaneously as an officer of any insured depository institution.
9: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to prohibit an insured depository institution from being or becoming an affiliate of any insurance company
Makes certain technical and conforming amendments to the Banking Act of 1933.

topic.name: Border Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing security and enforcement measures along the U.S. borders, particularly the southern border with Mexico.

Document 1: Directs the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to hire, train, and assign to duty, by September 30, 2020: 5,000 additional full-time U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) officers
Authorize CBP to aid in the enforcement of federal customs, immigration, and agriculture laws.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Defense (DOD) to deploy at least 6,000 National Guard personnel to perform operations in the Southwest Border region
Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to increase, by September 30, 2016, the number of Border Patrol agents stationed in the Region by 5,000.
Document 3: Authorizes the Governor of Arizona to order 3,000 members of the Arizona National Guard to perform Active Guard and Reserve duty along the Tucson Sector border
Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to increase, by January 1, 2015, the number of Border Patrol agents stationed along the Tucson Sector and the Yuma Sector borders by 3,000.
Document 4: Sets forth border security and enforcement provisions, including provisions respecting use of Department of Defense (DOD) surveillance equipment
Personnel increases and a national border security plan.
Document 5: This bill amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996 with respect to construction of border fencing
Direct the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to complete the required 700-mile southwest border fencing.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to hire, train, and deploy 2,000 additional Border Patrol agents along the southwest border
Amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996.
Document 7: Directs the Assistant Secretary of Homeland Security for the Transportation Security Administration to increase the number of federal air marshals
Ensures increased transportation security for inbound international flights.
Document 8: Directs the Assistant Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) for the Transportation Security Administration (TSA) to increase the number of federal air marshals
Goal is to increase the number of inbound flights with federal marshals onboard.
Document 9: Directs the Assistant Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) for the Transportation Security Administration (TSA) to increase the number of federal air marshals
Ensures increased transportation security for inbound international flights.
Document 10: Directs the Assistant Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) for the Transportation Security Administration (TSA) to increase the number of federal air marshals
Goal is to increase the number of inbound flights with federal marshals onboard.

topic.name: Drug and Food Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating drug approvals, food safety, and the management of substances to protect public health.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to prohibit a holder of a new, approved drug application from commencing to manufacture, market, sell, or distribute a generic version of such drug...
Document 2: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) to require the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to promulgate regulations...
Document 3: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to provide that, in determining whether a controlled substance analogue was intended for human consumption...
Document 4: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to prohibit a holder of a new, approved drug application from commencing to manufacture, market, sell, or distribute a generic version of such drug...
Document 5: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to prohibit a holder of a new, approved drug application from commencing to manufacture, market, sell, or distribute a generic version of such drug...
Document 6: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to deny an application for a new animal drug...
Document 7: Deems a food to be adulterated if its container: (1) is composed, in whole or in part, of bisphenol A...
Document 8: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to deny an application for a new animal drug...
Document 9: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to provide that, in determining whether a controlled substance analogue was intended for human consumption...
Document 10: Prohibits the distribution of a food if its container is composed, in whole or in part, of bisphenol A (BPA)...

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts on Labor and Welfare
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislative acts related to labor practices, animal welfare, and the recognition of historical figures in government.

Document 1: Wear American Act of 2014 - Directs the Federal Acquisition Regulation Council to amend the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) to require federal agencies to procure textiles and apparel articles, including components for such articles, that are manufactured in the United States.
Document 2: Amends the Animal Welfare Act to define a 'high volume retail breeder' as a person who, in commerce, for compensation or profit: (1) has an ownership interest in or custody of one or more breeding female dogs; and (2) sells, via any means of conveyance, more than 50 of the offspring of such dogs for use as pets in any one-year period.
Document 4: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to eliminate the consumptive demand exception to the prohibition against importation of goods manufactured by convict, forced, or indentured labor.
Document 6: Directs the Postal Service to provide for the issuance and sale of a semipostal in order to afford the public a convenient means to contribute towards the acquisition (for public display) of works of art honoring Jeanette Rankin, Mary Teresa Norton, Patsy Mink, Shirley Anita St. Hill Chisholm, and other female pioneers in government service.
Document 9: Requires any flags of the United States acquired for use by the federal government to be entirely manufactured in the United States from articles, materials, or supplies entirely grown, produced, or manufactured in the United States.

topic.name: Small Business Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative acts aimed at enhancing support, funding, and procurement opportunities for small businesses in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Investment Act of 1958 to increase... the maximum amount of outstanding leverage...
Small Business Administration (SBA) to two or more commonly controlled small business investment companies not under capital impairment.
Document 2: Amends the Small Business Act with respect to the procurement program for women-owned small businesses...
Allow a contracting officer to award a sole source contract to any economically disadvantaged women-owned small business...
Document 3: Amends the Small Business Act to increase... the maximum loan amount under the Small Business Administration (SBA) microloan program...
loans to start-up, newly established, or growing small businesses.
Document 4: Establishes a three-year small business intermediary lending pilot program...
for the purpose of making loans of up to $200,000 each to startup, newly-established, and growing small businesses.
Document 5: Amends the Small Business Act to make small businesses owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans eligible...
for the award by the Small Business Administration (SBA) of small business development grants.
Document 6: Amends the Small Business Act with respect to the HUBZone program to authorize...
a contracting opportunity to be awarded under the program on the basis of competition restricted to qualified HUBZone small businesses...
Document 7: Amends the Small Business Act to increase the government-wide goal for procurement contracts...
awarded to small businesses owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans, qualified HUBZone small businesses...
Document 8: Amends the Small Business Act to increase the government-wide goal for awards to small businesses...
from 23% to 24% of the total value of all federal prime procurement contracts...
Document 9: Amends the Small Business Act with respect to the HUBZone program...
Includes within HUBZone federal procurement small business contract participation goals...
Document 10: Amends the Office of Federal Procurement Policy Act to direct...
to establish a training certification program for federal contracting officers regarding small business government contracting set-aside programs.

topic.name: Homeownership Support Programs
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at facilitating homeownership through refinancing and mortgage modifications.

Document 1: Preserving American Homeownership Act of 2012 - Requires the Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency and the Federal Housing Commissioner each to establish a pilot program to encourage, through assistance provided under the Home Affordable Modification Program (HAMP)...
shared appreciation mortgage modifications that are designed to return greater cash flow to investors than other loss-mitigation activities, including foreclosure.
Document 2: Directs the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac) to establish a voluntary program for borrowers who qualify for the Home Affordable Refinance Program...
the GSE shall pay for the closing costs associated with applying for and receiving the refinancing when the borrower agrees to refinance into a fully amortizing loan.
Document 5: Preserving American Homeownership Act of 2013 - Requires the Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency and the Federal Housing Commissioner each to establish a pilot program to encourage...
shared appreciation mortgage modifications that result in positive net present value for the investor.
Document 6: Housing Opportunity and Mortgage Equity Act of 2011 - Directs the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac) to carry out a one-year program providing for the refinancing of qualified single-family housing mortgages...
Defines a qualified mortgage as one...that is an existing first mortgage for purchase of, or refinancing another first mortgage on, a one- to four-family dwelling.

topic.name: Land Exchange Legislation
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative acts concerning the exchange of federal and non-federal lands for conservation and public use purposes.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey to River Bottom Farms...
Requires the acquired non-federal land to become a part of the Cibola Wildlife Refuge.
Document 3: This bill directs the Department of Agriculture (USDA), if South Dakota conveys to it four parcels of specified nonfederal lands...
The values of the federal and nonfederal lands to be exchanged shall be equal.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey to the city of Coffman Cove, Alaska, without consideration and by quitclaim deed...
Requires, if the city sells any part of such conveyed land... the city to pay to the Secretary an amount equal to the gross proceeds of the sale.
